title: If we die, I won't let the world die with us

replies.msg:
就算香港爆多次SARS，我都唔要全世界幫香港埋單

I remember what my English teacher said about SARS(severe acute respiratory syndrome), " putting 'severe' and 'acute' together to name the disease is redundant. Either one is sufficient."

Indeed, that is a sarcastic representation that HKSAR was unable to suppress the 2003's outbreak, causing 299 deaths in local; and believably HK was "one of the origins" of the worldwide outbreak at that moment.

However, more sarcastic is that later when we traced back the source of SARS virus, there bad been a mini SARS outbreak in Guangdong community late 2002, but China did not report the case to WHO, nor to HK; while a Guangdong traveller triggered HK's outbreak.

It is urgent to have a concrete document showing SARS spreading history in HKSAR and China, and the evidence that China tended to conceal the outbreak of SARS. Could anyone help to reveal the fact?
As what we see in Wuhan's outbreak today, the situation could be critical.

And I must raise the concern of hygiene practice and disease prevention to global.

#StandWithHongKong

https://twitter.com/dockerfiller/status/1213467908778553344?s=19

using both severe and acute is not redundant
acute vs chronic
severe vs mild

replies.msg:
'It is urgent to have a concrete document showing SARS spreading history in HKSAR and China, and the evidence that China tended to conceal the outbreak of SARS.'
Indeed redundant on using concrete document, just use proof.
Again, if China had done such act, you should have simply address as China had concealed the outbreak of SARS and may have the tendency of doing so with regards to the wuhan pneumonia.
Yet, for the global outbreak of SARS, the consequence HK paid was not less than that China paid.
